reasoning: While Milorad Dodik escalated secessionist rhetoric in mid-2024 following the UN resolution on Srebrenica—even announcing that a referendum 'will be held' and forming a working group for a 'peaceful dissociation' agreement—he consistently avoided setting a specific date. Crucially, the RS government withdrew the 'peaceful dissociation' proposal from the National Assembly's agenda in July 2024. News reports through early 2025 (including the major 'Republic Day' on Jan 9) lack any mention of a scheduled date, focusing instead on political maneuvering and the legal impossibility of the move. Given Dodik's decades-long history of using such threats as leverage without committing to a concrete calendar, and the absence of any new legislative action in early 2025, the likelihood of an official date being announced before July 1, 2025, is very low.
